preamble: The palato-alveolar affricate [tʃ] merges into the retroflex affricate [ʈʂ] in various Mandarin dialects, contributing to the establishment of the retroflex series.

examples.note: Stabilization of the retroflex series via merger with palato-alveolar sounds.

Part of the Mamean chain shift: Proto-Mayan *[r] > [t], *[t] > [tʃ], *[tʃ] > [ʈʂ], *[ʃ] > [ʂ]. The shift of *tʃ to ʈʂ is well-attested in the Mamean branch (Mam, Tectitec, Awakatec, Ixil) and the resulting retroflex affricates later diffused into neighboring Q'anjob'alan languages through areal contact.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
